items.title: Ripple's preliminary crypto asset provider license in Luxembourg upgraded to fully compliant
items.summary: The approval means Ripple is now fully MiCA-compliant for payments, financial institutions, corporates and businesses across all 30 European Economic Area countries.

items.title: Thousands of crypto wallets at risk from ‘Ill Bloom’ vulnerability: Coinspect
items.summary: Thousands of crypto wallets across multiple blockchains could be vulnerable due to weak recovery phrase generation.

items.title: GPT-5.5-Cyber built a zlib fuzzing lab in a day
items.summary: We’re running Patch the Planet, an ongoing collaboration with OpenAI that pairs Trail of Bits engineers directly with more than 30 open-source projects. Its goal is to front-run a serious problem facing open-source maintainers: highly capable models like GPT-5.5-Cyber will soon c

items.title: Bitcoin's recent macro relief faces a challenge from Japanese interest rates
items.summary: Japanese bond yields continue to rise, lifting their U.S. counterparts and posing a potential headwind for risk assets.

items.title: SpaceX's Nasdaq 100 inclusion comes with a historical warning
items.summary: The largest IPO ever enters the Nasdaq 100. Previous additions like Palantir and Strategy saw volatility after the index inclusion rather than the start of new rallies.

items.title: Bitcoin's Sharpe Ratio slides to lowest since 2022. Here's what it means.
items.summary: A reading that negative means investors would have been better off in risk-free assets like 10-year U.S. Treasuries.

items.title: South Korea weighs action against Polymarket over gambling concerns
items.summary: South Korea’s media and communications review body will hear from Polymarket before deciding whether to issue a corrective request against the prediction market platform.

items.title: Binance taps into Bitcoin holders’ hunger for yield with new covered call yield play
items.summary: The product, called BTC Yield, is designed exclusively for people who already hold bitcoin.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin and ether ETFs drew inflows on Monday
items.summary: Spot bitcoin ETFs still lost a net $526.6 million over the shortened holiday week, an eighth straight week of negative flows.

items.title: BONK faces $20 million treasury drain after attacker spends $4 million to pass malicious proposal
items.summary: The attacker bought enough of the memecoin's tokens to pass a governance proposal that sent the group's holdings to a wallet they controlled, then began selling.

items.title: XRP stalls near $1.14 as breakout attempt struggles for volume
items.summary: Buyers defended the session lows and pushed XRP back toward resistance, but muted overall volume left traders waiting for confirmation above $1.13-$1.14.

items.title: Bitcoin drops after a run at $64,000, shrugging off Strategy's $213 million BTC sale
items.summary: The token touched $64,400 overnight before easing back, still up about 6% on the week. A fresh missile strike on a Qatari gas ship in the Strait of Hormuz lifted oil and tested the late-June peace deal as Asian tech sold off again.

items.title: Bitcoin can fall below $58K if one of its 'cleanest' metrics copies history: Analysis
items.summary: Bitcoin NUPL metric data suggested that BTC price action should make new cycle lows in order to preserve historical patterns.

items.title: Digital Chamber amicus brief urges dismissal of NY lawsuit over 39,069 Bitcoin wallets
items.summary: The Digital Chamber filed an amicus brief urging the dismissal of the New York lawsuit seeking ownership of 39,069 dormant Bitcoin wallets, arguing it would set a dangerous precedent for self-custodial wallets.

items.title: MiCA-compliant euro stablecoins grew 128% before MiCA transition ended, says Decta
items.summary: Decta's report said the market cap of eight MiCA-compliant euro stablecoins rose to $673.9 million in the year before Europe’s CASP transition period ended.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Trader loses $2M in ‘same-block backrun extraction’ exploit
items.summary: One crypto trader noted the $2 million loss could have been prevented had the victim read the transaction route before signing the transaction.

items.title: Yield Guild Games cuts 35 staff, shuts game publisher to focus on AI
items.summary: Yield Guild Games said tough crypto and video game market conditions mean its YGG Play business is no longer commercially sustainable.

items.title: Strategy’s Bitcoin sale may give BTC a ‘durable bottom,’ Grayscale says
items.summary: “Investors are now more confident about the instrument,” said Grayscale’s Zach Pandl regarding STRC, which reclaimed $90 for the first time in three weeks.

items.title: Trump says he became ‘a big crypto guy’ partly for politics
items.summary: US President Donald Trump once called Bitcoin “a scam,” but admitted on Monday that he got involved in crypto “a little bit for politics.”
